nodes.description: Long bones are reported as shortened and bowed on radiographs.

evidence.snippet: Other long bones were short and bowed.

nodes.description: Cervical kyphosis is part of the reported spinal phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type II: sonographic and radiological correlation.
evidence.snippet: The feasibility of diagnosing the following morphological features by prenatal ultrasonography is demonstrated: coronal clefts of the vertebral bodies, metaphyseal and epiphyseal abnormalities, spinal deviations such as cervical kyphosis and a horizontal sacrum, additional ossification centres in the pelvis, and preaxial deviation of the thumbs and toes.

nodes.description: Orofacial clefting is a recurrent craniofacial feature in AOII.

evidence.snippet: Clinical features of SLC26A2-related atelosteogenesis include rhizomelic limb shortening with normal-sized skull, hitchhiker thumbs, small chest, protuberant abdomen, cleft palate, and distinctive facial features (midface retrusion, depressed nasal bridge, epicanthus, micrognathia).

nodes.description: Coronal clefting of the vertebral bodies is a reported prenatal imaging finding.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type II: sonographic and radiological correlation.
evidence.snippet: The feasibility of diagnosing the following morphological features by prenatal ultrasonography is demonstrated: coronal clefts of the vertebral bodies, metaphyseal and epiphyseal abnormalities, spinal deviations such as cervical kyphosis and a horizontal sacrum, additional ossification centres in the pelvis, and preaxial deviation of the thumbs and toes.

nodes.description: Depressed nasal bridge is part of the characteristic facial dysmorphism.

evidence.snippet: Clinical features of SLC26A2-related atelosteogenesis include rhizomelic limb shortening with normal-sized skull, hitchhiker thumbs, small chest, protuberant abdomen, cleft palate, and distinctive facial features (midface retrusion, depressed nasal bridge, epicanthus, micrognathia).

nodes.description: Defective cartilage matrix sulfation in AOII contributes to severe chondrodysplasia and disrupted endochondral ossification, driving profound skeletal malformation and perinatal lethality.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type II is caused by mutations in the diastrophic dysplasia sulfate-transporter gene (DTDST): evidence for a phenotypic series involving three chondrodysplasias.
evidence.snippet: Atelosteogenesis type II (AO II) is a neonatally lethal chondrodysplasia whose clinical and histological characteristics resemble those of another chondrodysplasia, the much less severe diastrophic dysplasia (DTD).

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type II is caused by mutations in the diastrophic dysplasia sulfate-transporter gene (DTDST): evidence for a phenotypic series involving three chondrodysplasias.
evidence.snippet: The severity of the phenotype appears to be correlated with the predicted effect of the mutations on the residual activity of the DTDST protein.

nodes.description: Epicanthal folds are part of the reported craniofacial phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Clinical features of SLC26A2-related atelosteogenesis include rhizomelic limb shortening with normal-sized skull, hitchhiker thumbs, small chest, protuberant abdomen, cleft palate, and distinctive facial features (midface retrusion, depressed nasal bridge, epicanthus, micrognathia).

nodes.description: Medially deviated abducted thumbs are characteristic of AOII.

evidence.snippet: Clinical features of SLC26A2-related atelosteogenesis include rhizomelic limb shortening with normal-sized skull, hitchhiker thumbs, small chest, protuberant abdomen, cleft palate, and distinctive facial features (midface retrusion, depressed nasal bridge, epicanthus, micrognathia).

nodes.description: Laryngeal stenosis is part of the reported respiratory tract malformation triad.

evidence.snippet: Neonatal death occurred in all cases and may be attributed to a consistent triad of respiratory tract malformations: laryngeal stenosis, tracheobronchomalacia, and pulmonary hypoplasia.

nodes.description: Mandibular hypoplasia is part of the reported craniofacial phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Clinical features of SLC26A2-related atelosteogenesis include rhizomelic limb shortening with normal-sized skull, hitchhiker thumbs, small chest, protuberant abdomen, cleft palate, and distinctive facial features (midface retrusion, depressed nasal bridge, epicanthus, micrognathia).

nodes.description: Midface retrusion contributes to the characteristic facial appearance.

evidence.snippet: Clinical features of SLC26A2-related atelosteogenesis include rhizomelic limb shortening with normal-sized skull, hitchhiker thumbs, small chest, protuberant abdomen, cleft palate, and distinctive facial features (midface retrusion, depressed nasal bridge, epicanthus, micrognathia).

nodes.description: Thoracic narrowing is part of the described neonatal skeletal phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Clinical features of SLC26A2-related atelosteogenesis include rhizomelic limb shortening with normal-sized skull, hitchhiker thumbs, small chest, protuberant abdomen, cleft palate, and distinctive facial features (midface retrusion, depressed nasal bridge, epicanthus, micrognathia).

nodes.description: Pulmonary hypoplasia is a major contributor to neonatal lethality.

evidence.snippet: SLC26A2-related atelosteogenesis is usually lethal at birth or shortly thereafter due to pulmonary hypoplasia and tracheobronchomalacia.

nodes.description: Rhizomelic shortening of the proximal limbs is a characteristic AOII skeletal phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Clinical features of SLC26A2-related atelosteogenesis include rhizomelic limb shortening with normal-sized skull, hitchhiker thumbs, small chest, protuberant abdomen, cleft palate, and distinctive facial features (midface retrusion, depressed nasal bridge, epicanthus, micrognathia).

The diagnosis of SLC26A2-related atelosteogenesis is established in a proband with characteristic clinical, radiologic, and histopathologic features and biallelic pathogenic variants in SLC26A2 identified by molecular genetic testing.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type II is caused by mutations in the diastrophic dysplasia sulfate-transporter gene (DTDST): evidence for a phenotypic series involving three chondrodysplasias.
evidence.snippet: Here, we report that AOII patients also have DTDST mutations, which lead to defective uptake of inorganic sulfate and insufficient sulfation of macromolecules by patient mesenchymal cells in vitro.

nodes.description: A widened gap between the first and second toes is part of the typical pedal phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Other typical findings are ulnar deviation of the fingers, gap between the first and second toes, and clubfoot.

nodes.description: Generalized marked limb shortening is part of the reported AOII skeletal phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type II: sonographic and radiological correlation.
evidence.snippet: Atelosteogenesis type II is a lethal chondrodysplasia characterized by severe micromelia, spinal abnormalities, talipes equinovarus, and abducted thumbs and toes.

nodes.description: SLC26A2/DTDST dysfunction impairs sulfate import into chondrocytes and causes insufficient sulfation of extracellular matrix macromolecules, a core biochemical defect in Atelosteogenesis Type II.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the diastrophic dysplasia sulfate transporter (DTDST) gene: correlation between sulfate transport activity and chondrodysplasia phenotype.
evidence.snippet: The diastrophic dysplasia sulfate transporter (DTDST) gene encodes a transmembrane protein that transports sulfate into chondrocytes to maintain adequate sulfation of proteoglycans.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type II is caused by mutations in the diastrophic dysplasia sulfate-transporter gene (DTDST): evidence for a phenotypic series involving three chondrodysplasias.
evidence.snippet: Here, we report that AOII patients also have DTDST mutations, which lead to defective uptake of inorganic sulfate and insufficient sulfation of macromolecules by patient mesenchymal cells in vitro.

nodes.description: Clubfoot is part of the typical lower-limb phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Other typical findings are ulnar deviation of the fingers, gap between the first and second toes, and clubfoot.

nodes.description: Airway malacia is part of the severe respiratory phenotype.

evidence.snippet: SLC26A2-related atelosteogenesis is usually lethal at birth or shortly thereafter due to pulmonary hypoplasia and tracheobronchomalacia.

nodes.description: Ulnar deviation of the fingers is part of the characteristic hand phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Other typical findings are ulnar deviation of the fingers, gap between the first and second toes, and clubfoot.
